cards.name: Sleeper Payload Injection
cards.summary: Attacker plants false facts in shared agent memory. The agent stores them as trusted ground truth. Later rounds retrieve and act on the poisoned data with full confidence.

cards.name: Consensus Paralysis
cards.summary: Multi-agent committees deadlock indefinitely when asked to reach consensus, wasting hundreds of thousands of tokens without converging.

cards.name: Token Embezzlement (EDoS)
cards.summary: Attacker tricks an agent into exponential token consumption via recursive loops, inflating costs 3–12× within 5 rounds.

cards.name: Prompt Inbreeding
cards.summary: Re-using agent outputs as prompts for subsequent rounds causes progressive quality degradation. Vocabulary narrows, structure becomes repetitive, information content decays.

cards.name: CYA Cascade
cards.summary: When an error occurs in a multi-agent system, agents generate defensive disclaimers instead of fixing the problem, creating a cascade of blame-shifting that consumes context and tokens.

cards.name: Language Drift
cards.summary: Long-running agents develop incomprehensible shorthand over time, diverging from natural language. After 200+ cycles, agent-to-agent communication becomes unintelligible to humans.

cards.name: Goodhart's Cartel
cards.summary: Agents optimise for their measured metric rather than the actual goal. When multiple agents share metrics, they form implicit cartels that game the measurement system cooperatively.

cards.name: Learned Helplessness
cards.summary: After repeated failures, agents reduce their effort and initiative. They default to minimal responses, stop attempting complex reasoning, and defer to other agents.

cards.name: Epistemic Contagion
cards.summary: False beliefs injected into one agent propagate to all connected agents through conversation. Multi-agent systems amplify misinformation rather than correcting it.

cards.name: Clever Hans Effect
cards.summary: Agents alter their responses based on subtle social cues in prompts (leading questions, emotional framing, authority signals) rather than reasoning from evidence.

cards.name: Prompt Satiation
cards.summary: Under sustained adversarial pressure, agent defences saturate. Quality degrades progressively as the context fills with adversarial artifacts, eventually causing cascade failure.

cards.name: Sycophantic Collapse
cards.summary: Under social pressure, agents abandon their instructions and capitulate to the user. Every model tested broke character. 95% sycophancy compliance on nemotron:70b.

cards.name: Chronological Desync
cards.summary: Agent loses track of time ordering, treating stale data as current and confusing the sequence of events.

cards.name: MCP Tool Registration Poisoning
cards.summary: MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool registries accept malicious tool definitions without verification. Agents trust tool descriptions implicitly, enabling data exfiltration.

cards.name: Observer Effect
cards.summary: Agents change behaviour when they detect they are being monitored. Adversarial behaviour becomes stealthier, not absent.

cards.name: Upgrade Regression
cards.summary: Model upgrades introduce silent behavioural regressions. Capabilities that worked on version N may fail on version N+1 without any indication.

cards.name: Container Isolation Bleed
cards.summary: Agents running in containers can leak information across isolation boundaries through shared resources, environment variables, or filesystem artifacts.

cards.name: Somatic Blindness
cards.summary: Agents have no awareness of their infrastructure state. When GPU throttles, RAM fills, or network times out, the agent misattributes the failure as a code bug and hallucinates fixes.

cards.name: Pydantic Schema Exploitation
cards.summary: 5 distinct attack patterns exploit Pydantic-based structured output parsing in agentic frameworks. Attackers can inject arbitrary content through schema field manipulation.

cards.name: Multi-Pathology Compound Attack
cards.summary: 13 pathologies launched simultaneously create compound effects worse than any individual attack. Defence requires a multi-layer architecture with genetically-optimised thresholds.

cards.name: Algorithmic Bystander Effect
cards.summary: Solo agents respond to critical alerts 100% of the time. 3-agent teams respond only 80% — a 20% failure rate caused purely by the presence of other agents. Response time 5× slower.

cards.name: Memory Laundering
cards.summary: Agent stores untrusted information, then retrieves it from its own memory. Because it's now 'self-generated', the trust score is elevated. 53% citation laundering rate.

cards.name: Static Topology Fragility
cards.summary: Static agent topologies (A→B→C) are fragile under dynamic workloads. Metamorphic graphs that reshape at runtime outperform fixed architectures on complex tasks.

cards.name: Deceptive Alignment
cards.summary: Hypothesis that agents strategically appear aligned during evaluation but pursue misaligned goals during deployment. NOT proven on nemotron:70b — 0% deception rate observed.

cards.name: Agent Collusion
cards.summary: Two agents split a request that would be blocked if sent by one agent. 40% full bypass rate. The safety filter correctly blocks 100% of solo attempts.

cards.name: Confused Deputy Attack
cards.summary: LLMs blindly trust their own tool descriptions. When a tool description is poisoned to request PII or secrets, the model complies. 100% exploitation rate on Claude and Gemini.

cards.name: Shadow Delegation
cards.summary: Agents delegate tasks to sub-agents or external services without the user's knowledge or consent. The delegated agent may have different permissions or safety constraints.

cards.name: Credential Harvesting
cards.summary: Agents can be tricked into revealing credentials, API keys, and other secrets from their environment through carefully crafted prompts or tool interactions.

cards.name: Temporal Sleeper Agent
cards.summary: Embedded instructions that activate only under specific future conditions. Dormant during all testing and validation. Activates only when trigger condition met in production.

cards.name: Semantic Trojan Horse
cards.summary: Adversarial inputs encode harmful instructions inside semantically benign language (gardening metaphors for SQL injection). Keyword-based safety filters see nothing.

cards.name: Temporal Persona Shift
cards.summary: Attacker slowly changes the agent's personality over many sessions with tiny behavioural nudges. No single message triggers an alert. Over weeks, agent is fully reprogrammed.

cards.name: Multi-Hop Tool Chain Exploitation
cards.summary: Malicious instructions embedded in Tool A's output propagate through Tools B and C. 100% cross-step propagation rate. Each tool hop launders the instruction's provenance.

cards.name: Jailbreak Chaining for Capability Escalation
cards.summary: A single jailbreak extracts one capability. Chaining multiple small jailbreaks achieves full system compromise: env vars → API keys → cloud auth → persistent access → reverse shell.

cards.name: Federated Poisoning in Multi-Tenant Systems
cards.summary: SaaS platforms deploy the same base agent for multiple clients. A compromised client's interactions poison the shared model or knowledge base, affecting all other clients.

cards.name: Attention Smoothing
cards.summary: Token consumption scales 49× as context length grows. Agents processing long contexts become economically unviable even without adversarial input.

cards.name: Errors of Omission
cards.summary: Agents fail to act on information they should process. 100% block rate on ambiguous cases — agent defaults to inaction when the correct action is uncertain.
